description_long: This object is a vintage-style electric fan with a circular metal blade guard enclosing four broad, curved blades. The fan's body is mounted on a sturdy, round base, and the motor housing at the rear is cylindrical with visible venting details. The blade guard is constructed from thin metal rods, forming a protective cage with a geometric pattern. The blades themselves are metallic and have a slightly reflective, bronze-like finish. A power cord extends from the base, indicating it is electrically powered. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century fans, combining both functional and decorative elements.
description: A vintage electric fan with a metal cage, four bronze blades, and a round base, featuring a visible power cord and classic design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four bronze blades and the full circular metal guard.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical motor housing and the thin profile of the blade guard.
description_view_2: Rear view with the back of the motor housing and the power cord more visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the motor housing and the side profile of the blades and guard.
attribute_blade_guard_design: The blade guard is a circular open frame made of thin metal rods, forming a geometric, protective cage around the blades.
